title: BIO 40A — Week 8 Muscles
description: Skeletal muscle tissue & axial muscles — 78 questions generated from the Week 8 terms list.

questions.prompt: Which term matches this description: “Long, cylindrical organelle that runs parallel within the muscle fiber and contains the sarcomeres”?
questions.explanation: Myofibrils: long, cylindrical organelle that runs parallel within the muscle fiber and contains the sarcomeres.

questions.prompt: Which best describes the Myofibrils?
options: Long, cylindrical organelle that runs parallel within the muscle fiber and contains the sarcomeres
Specialized smooth endoplasmic reticulum, which stores, releases, and retrieves Ca++
Plasma membrane of a skeletal muscle fiber
Longitudinally, repeating functional unit of skeletal muscle, with all of the contractile and associated proteins involved in contraction
questions.explanation: Myofibrils: long, cylindrical organelle that runs parallel within the muscle fiber and contains the sarcomeres.

questions.prompt: Which term matches this description: “Longitudinally, repeating functional unit of skeletal muscle, with all of the contractile and associated proteins involved in contraction”?
questions.explanation: Sarcomere: longitudinally, repeating functional unit of skeletal muscle, with all of the contractile and associated proteins involved in contraction.

questions.prompt: Which best describes the Sarcomere?

Long, cylindrical organelle that runs parallel within the muscle fiber and contains the sarcomeres
Plasma membrane of a skeletal muscle fiber
Specialized smooth endoplasmic reticulum, which stores, releases, and retrieves Ca++
questions.explanation: Sarcomere: longitudinally, repeating functional unit of skeletal muscle, with all of the contractile and associated proteins involved in contraction.

questions.prompt: Which term matches this description: “Thick, fan-shaped axial muscle that covers much of the superior thorax”?

options: Thick, fan-shaped axial muscle that covers much of the superior thorax
Superficial abdominal muscle with fascicles that extend inferiorly and medially
Muscle that moves the scapula and assists in inhalation
Long, linear muscle that extends along the middle of the trunk
Large and flat muscle that originates on the ribs and inserts onto the scapula
